title: rumex pulcher subsp. woodsii
intro: Rumex pulcher subsp. woodsii, also known as the Wood's dock, is a subspecies of the perennial dock plant native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its high levels of anthraquinones, such as emodin, that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rumex pulcher subsp. woodsii include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, constipation, and skin irritations.
